abstract:
This study examines the information content of textual disclosures in firms’ earnings announcements. Using a large language model (LLM) to capture information in both words and word context, I show that the news in earnings press releases (i) explains three times more variation in short-window stock returns than a host of textual measures based on dictionary and non-LLM machine learning methods; (ii) doubles the R
                    2
                    of an array of financial statement surprises, modeled with conventional regression or machine learning approaches; and (iii) accounts for a large fraction of immediate price revisions within just five minutes of release. LLM-modeled conference calls further enhance R
                    2
                    by one fourth compared with press releases and financial surprises. Textual disclosures are more informative when earnings are less persistent and during periods of aggregate uncertainty. Most news arises from text describing numbers, at the beginning of the disclosure, and including novel contents. These findings highlight the role of firms’ textual disclosures in moving stock prices and advance our understanding of how investors utilize corporate disclosures.
                  
                  This paper was accepted by Suraj Srinivasan, accounting.
                  Funding: The author gratefully acknowledges financial support from the Naveen Jindal School of Management.
                  Supplemental Material: The online appendix and data files are available at https://doi.org/10.1287/mnsc.2024.05417 .

affiliation: The University of Texas at Dallas
authors_full.name: Federico Siano

ob.ai_reason: 使用LLM方法分析财报文本，AI为核心研究工具
ob.tldr: 利用大语言模型捕捉财报公告中词语与词境的文本信息。研究表明，LLM建模的文本信息对短期窗口股票收益的解释力是字典法及非LLM机器学习方法的3倍；与财务惊喜结合后R²提升一倍；在发布后5分钟内即解释大量即时价格调整。文本信息披露在盈利持续性较低和市场整体不确定性较高时期信息含量更高。新闻主要来自描述数字、位于披露开头及包含新内容的文本。

title: The News in Earnings Announcement Disclosures: Capturing Word Context Using LLM Methods
title_zh: 盈利公告披露中的新闻：基于LLM方法捕捉词汇语境
